transcript.text: Time is a construct when you're working at an early stage startup, as I'm sure you understand.
But great to meet you and Grace.
Hey, Grace, how's it going?
Good.
How are you?
Yeah, doing really well.
Always happy on a Friday.
So Ankit, I don't have too much context just to be totally transparent with you.
Could you maybe just like catch me up to speed as we both, you know, go through introductions and we can start with you and then move to Grace after?

Yeah, so just think of this as, see, last time we chatted, so I've been in touch with Marcel since like a year almost, because I saw his workshops when he used to run those organic growth workshops, right?
At that time, I think probably he didn't even think of starting a company.
But then later, yeah, I think he saw the demand and he jumped on the wagon.
And then I was a part of two of those.
And then when the company started, I was definitely tracking his progress and I was impressed.
And then last year in July, June, around that time, we were busy with our Snowflake conference and we wanted to start investing in programmatic SEO.
And we thought of GrowthX, obviously, and I recommended internally.
And the conversation started with the CEO internally and with you guys externally.
And I had one call.
So that's all the history, right?
But then what happened is it didn't fall off and I dropped the ball because we were going through some internal restructuring.
And then I was like, let's just go through that first and then we will get back on track and then get in touch, touch base with GrowthX again.
So here we are, And touch basing again.
And our situation is like, I think, in an optimal track for GrowthX collaboration as of now.
So I think from here, yeah, let me know if you have any questions.
Otherwise, you'll just go with what Grace has to ask.
Yeah, I can also.
Yeah.
I
Quick introduction myself.
I'm Grace.
I'm part of marketing team, and Ankit, myself, lead our marketing effort here.
And just a little bit of background of the company.
You may know about us, founded by Andrew Ng, one of AI pioneers, about seven, eight years ago.
And we started more of a computer vision, visual inspection business with our platform.
About six, eight months ago, we introduced a document extraction product, and it really hit the market, received very well from the market.
And we really pivoted our company as now positioning industry leader in document extractions.
So now we are in the process of updating website, like refreshing all the positioning and contents.
And you may see the updated website in the next two months or so, but we wanted to also continue to drive the effort, like all the growth effort around like document extraction learn.
So that's why we are here too.
I think this is great timing for us to build a plan and see how we can attack the market better.
So that's why we are here today.
Super helpful.
Thanks for all the contacts.
And just as a quick intro, Grace, I'm the founding AE at GrowthX.
So I work hand in hand with Marcel and, you know, my number one job is to free up time on his calendar.

So he can, you know, do things like launch the two products that we're working on that should be ready in Q1 and also focus on, right, strategic work with some of our biggest clients like Brex, Ramp, Webflow.
So, so that's just a little bit of context on us.
Um,
In terms of growthx, Ankit, I know you know all about us, but Grace, do you have like an introduction to us or would it be helpful?
Yeah, it would be helpful.
Perfect.
Yeah, let's do that.
So I'm going to grab this deck here.
So what's great about our process is it's really focused on SEO and GEO, right?
So Monograph is one of our customers and like the traditional search, it's more like, you you're targeting keywords.
It's software for X, Y, Z.
And then AI search, a lot more nuanced.
The first sales conversation is pretty much happening here.
And it's almost like if you're not in the answer, you don't exist.
And so the benefit and why GrowthX has experienced a lot of traction is we can help teams get in the answer by creating quality at scale.
So we typically work on editorial content first and we're end-to-end.
It's like strategy, content creation, we'll stage and publish in your CMS.
We'll deliver all the LLM visibility and monitoring.
And the goal is, right, if you can get in the answer, you can drive traffic that's more likely to convert, that's more qualified and well-informed, that are likely to spend longer on the site.

And so, you know, our process, I'll show you, like, the workflows that we leverage, but it's really sophisticated AI workflows and experts in the loop, making sure that every piece of content is going through these, like, most important factors for GEO.
So how is the content structured?
How is the research done?
Can you fact-check and validate everything that's been written?
Is the content refreshed?
Like, we recommend every three months, refreshing content.
And so that's a little bit on our process.
In terms of Marcel, I think the story is really interesting and differentiates us.
He has always, like, been a build-in public and organic content type of marketing leader.
He's never spent much on paid.
He's got, like, a story of a content engineer.
He aggregated at each stop in his journey, but the highlights are just that he took HashiCorp from $6 to $100 million on the marketing side.
He was with Service Titan as a market leader through their hypergrowth, and he was with Scale as they got acquired by Meta.

And then at Deepgram, what he did is he replicated that process he followed at other companies, but this time spending thousands of hours in AI workflow orchestration tools, like figuring out like, how can I automate the process of building a really robust brief of thinking through the right assignments to create content for, to even like creating the content, including like deep research fact checking.
So, yeah, that's a little long-winded, but hopefully that gives you some good context on GrowthX and why people are enjoying working with us.
Any questions?
Yeah.
So I know you touch upon AEO, SEO side of the world, but I know you...
You guys also offer ProScope spectrum of the growth.
So it will be helpful type of service that you offer and where, yeah, it seems like AO is bread and butter, but where you see also some success from the others, I think that will be also helpful.
Yeah, it makes sense.
You know, we've been doing a lot of work since I joined to think about scalability and delivering quality for every new client that we bring on.
So we are starting off with every new client on editorial content, right?
That doesn't mean just the blogs.
Like we've done all these different guides for augment code and these guides, you know, they're more like comparisons or thought leadership around AI coding.
You'll see that in a lot of this content, we've got like code snippets that we build into what we're writing.
But I'm curious what you're interested in, like outside of editorial or programmatic.
feel like that?
of Yeah, I'm I Well, but
Because that's typically why most clients are hiring us today and I think what we're trying to focus on with our services.
Yeah, I think that's definitely one of the main interests of us, but I just asked to understand the scope of work and service that you offer.
And so we can discuss where is the best leverage for us to have you guys.
So Tyler, I'll share my screen and I'll elaborate on what Grace is asking exactly by showing you the instance from your website, okay?
Oh yeah, understood.
Yeah, our website, it definitely says more than like it should right now.
We're not doing everything that you're seeing on the website and just how to make sure we update that.
Good to know.
Link building, you do that, right?
We're not focused on like doing backlinking unless it's...
Like, later in the partnership.
So we wouldn't do that to start.
Okay.
What about social media?
Although, yeah.
Yeah, same thing.
Okay.
really any...
So it's fine.
Same thing for anything outside of, like, editorial and programmatic, to be honest with you.
Okay.
No paid media?
Just go double-checking.

And no ongoing experiential lead generation on sales.
Nothing there.

How later are we talking?
Oh, okay.
So off for six months.
We can think about, you know, more custom work streams after the first editorial foundation that we've built.
But really think of us, Ankit, as, like, your partner to create content at scale.
And I appreciate you, you know, bringing up the website because this will be...
And...
Yeah.
Good for me to use internally to express why this copy needs to be updated.
Awesome.

And I obviously didn't see this in the last iteration.
So I was like, okay, well, let's just double check.
So that's why Grace brought it.
Okay.
Yeah.
Yeah, no, I'm sorry for that.

Okay.
Let's see.
Yep.
One thing again, man.
See, in the world of SEO, GEO, yeah.

I would like to know what your offerings are and where does they start?
Because I think that.
There's some increase in the price as well.
Yeah, absolutely.
Let's start there.
So we are doing an eight-week strategy sprint and then 12-month growth execution for startups, right?
30K for the strategy sprint, 15K per month for the growth execution.
In terms of what you get from us, you get everything you see here in the strategy sprint and also at least five, usually more like 10, articles that we publish in your CMS.
And then in the growth execution, we target five net new or refreshed articles per week.
And so with the price, this would represent the editorial.
And then we could swap that out for programmatic without increasing cost.
But if you wanted us to perform one of these other work streams, which, right, like they're more case-by-case.
They're more custom.
Think about it as we've built out a newsletter for some of our clients.
There's one client I think we did backlinking for.
But just depending on the complexity of that work stream, it would be an additional $9,000 to $15,000 per month to add something to the editorial or programmatic starting point.
I see.
So in the sprint phase of eight weeks, you still go for five per week?
No, we would go for making sure that we align on quality, create artifacts that are going to give us the context engineering to be successful with our content creation.
It's building the machine and it's delivering the insights in terms of like technical SEO, creating the roadmap of assignments and the topic clusters that we're creating the content around.
And then it's like five to 10 articles by the end of phase one that you would get from our team.
So in eight weeks, max 10, min 5.
Case by case, you know, if the review process is super easy, we could probably get more than 10 done.
But in the first few articles that we create, there's typically a lot of back and forth and calibration to make sure we get to quality.
Makes sense.
How does the, what does that process looks like in terms of, see, I'm thinking about like the execution of it, right?

Like, for example, how many people are involved as strategists with their expertise and how many people are assigned for, let's say, executing some of those strategies or tactics in the initial growth sprint?
Yeah, for sure.
I can dig in there, but I'd really like love to hear first from you both on what specifically, because we've mentioned a lot of different use cases, like you feel like you would benefit from.
And also like, what kind of content are you ultimately looking for us to create?
Like, is it.
Is that your developers would write, or is it more content that's optimized for search?
Yeah, both.
So we have, as I mentioned, our product is agentic document extractions, the API product.
So main user of the product is a developer, especially enterprise that we are targeting, where they process a large volume of documents.
But we also need to create the contents targeting business decision maker who wanted to optimize their process and gain insight from their documents, more of a subject matter expert, right?
So that's the two angles that we wanted to have the contents to serve.
That makes sense.
Would you expect us to be like publishing to your blog or different places on the website?
Yeah, I think.
So that's why I also think it will be good.

Good to understand your scope of services, like we normally publish on our website and leverage other channels like Mediums and LinkedIn, but like also wanted to hear from you, other channels that you help to distribute those contents or is it more of a content creation and helps with the keywords.
Yeah, would say it's typically publishing on your marketing site and very rare that we're publishing to other platforms and yeah, that's typically where we're focused.
Ankit, on your question about the team, we have like a managing editor who would work end-to-end on the content creation.
We've got a team of like managing directors who are working with...

I see.
See, the thing is, how do you, like, let's say, so initial eight-week sprint is fine because you're just publishing five to ten.
Later, when you produce hundreds, then obviously it's not going to be on our blog.
So where does it go?

Slash guides is a place that we've published a lot.
we did.

So I'm saying, like, how do you claim to be able to rank us when you don't holistically handle all that?
Yeah, it's a valid question for sure.
And we definitely think, like, those other channels are important.
But with, like, B2B software specifically, we definitely believe and have, like, you know, read different studies.
you.
That talk about how your marketing side is like the best place to create content that's going to have an ROI when it comes to AI search.
I think, you know, if we try to do everything, then we might not do everything like at the highest caliber.
And so we've just found like that editorial and programmatic is where we can deliver like the most ROI at scale.
And that foundation is so important anyways to your success.
So if we can succeed there, you know, we want to be a long-term partner and we want to be like an extension of your team.
You know, people would be hiring growthx instead of hiring an SEO agency that just does strategy and maybe like creates briefs or creates some content.

With us, you know, we're able to do what building out a full team would be doing or we'd be able to, right, do what lots of our clients are very worried about trying to do in like an AI workflow orchestration tool.
Either Either be.
Because costs will scale out of control or the time to value will be a lot longer.
So, you know, not necessarily saying, like, this is the only thing you need to do to have success with AI search.
But we do believe it's the most important.
And that's why we focus there.
And metrics.
How do you evaluate that we're actually getting ranked?
Oh, yeah, for sure.

Connect, you know, to the conversion event.
We'll track that too.
And so, you know, the data is like pointing in the right direction in terms of our strategy, right?
Like, so we're using that and keeping you in the loop with every, you know, meeting that we have about how it's improving.
Do we need, what internal help do you need from us when you do those tracking?
Like, example, yeah, like, do you make sure that our blog is indexable properly?
Like our website is indexable and LLM searchable?

And I was like, what the heck is happening here?
So I had to optimize.
Yeah, for sure.
We do that in the strategy sprint.
So that's part of like the technical SEO audit that we do.
Nice.
Okay.
And what is the, I see in this strategy.
And then I think, I think I'll just add like the stage.

Talk about strategy a bit.

Yeah, for sure.
So I can actually show you like a quick example there.

we'll can third-zero,

And then we handle like that execution after the fact.
The biggest problem that I'm seeing is...
You will go with the eight-week sprint, and we will get the strategy as well as some artifacts.
Even when they are the highest quality, there's no way to evaluate the success we can expect in the next three months, especially given that you will have us in a binding contract for 12 months.
Is that right?
Yeah, that's fair.
It is like a leap of faith in building an organic content engine.
You're not going to see performance right away.
So what we try to do is we try to show you the quality that we can hit, which is always going to improve, and the roadmap that we would execute on.
And then from there, I think you have to trust our expertise, the clients we're working with, the examples that we've created to make the decision about whether you want to continue.
Grace, any question before I ask more?
Yeah, so what are some of the key differentiators of your service?
As compared to many other SEO and AO agencies in the market, what would you say what made you stood out compared to others?
Yeah, I would say, you know, probably nobody has more expertise in this space than our founder and CEO, maybe compared to others out there.
Got a track record of doing this as a CMO and marketing leader at several companies that had hypergrowth.
The sophistication of the workflows, the workflows, I can demo them for you on like a follow-up, but they are going super deep and can be customized from client to client.
And so I think those pieces and like the end-to-end nature of what we offer is different than other options.
How, how, Wapi has joined you, I think, right?
I know Jordan.
He was, before joining, landing.
joining now.
He tried to bring me on to his company.
So how is it going with WAPI?
I know how aggressive he is, so I'm curious to know.
Yeah, I'm not too sure to tell you the truth.
They joined before I got here, and I don't typically use the examples we create for them, but I can check with the team.
Sorry about that.
No worries.
Yeah, thanks for being honest.
Yeah, see, the thing is, you know, I understand the leap of faith and all.
Your track record is solid.
I'm not going to argue against it.

The thing is, what other, see, Grace always loves to see, and so do I, but especially Grace, I've noticed, the data points, like the case studies, like, so that we have, like, really strong evidence-based backing to justify internally to our leadership that, hey, this is the way to go, right?
She has worked with agencies before.
She has those data points, but how far can we have those data points from?
From you guys, that's what my question is.
Yeah, for sure.
We are like publishing our first couple of case studies.
We've been around for a year, right?
So like a lot of this stuff is in motion, but not out the door.
I've got some good proof points that I can send in an email.
So you can see like real evidence of like the impact we've made.

But also, you know, I think just kind of make sure that from the examples I share and what our conversation has been today, like you feel confident that where we would execute is like what you need and what would build the most ROI because we're like in a privileged position right now where we're not demand constrained and we're really trying to be smart about, you know, making sure we choose the right clients to work with.
But we also want to like help you do your due diligence and answer every question that you have.
So I can send that over to you.
And I'm happy to do a follow-up demo if you think it would be helpful and you think it would be.
Worth the time to dive deeper into the evaluation.
Let's do it.
Yeah.
But then there's one more question I have.
See, the problem is, see, the biggest problem or pain point other SEO companies that operate in this business would have is the content quality and thought leadership kind of content itself.
Because that's where internally we are the leaders.
We are the experts.
Agreed.
Yeah.
And nobody trusts anybody else to actually do it better than themselves.
Right?
So at least the point is, can you come up to the same level, if not more?
Right?
Yeah.
At least somewhere in the zone horizon.
So that's the question here.
Like, I mean, we can verify from whatever examples you show us, but how does that process look like of you getting up to speed with document understanding, intelligent document processing?
So
Or DocumentAI or OCR, because that's the area of, that's the space, that's a competitive space we're operating in.
And it's very, very, it's not exactly very niche.
It's API first, developer first thing that we have got.

But I'm saying like they're direct 10 competitors in the form of startups and direct five competitors in the form of big companies like Google, Amazon, Text Extract, and you have your Microsoft Cognition as well.

So I'm saying, how like, is it really realistic for us to expect that you would go deep enough and be able to like really pick up the domain knowledge and have a deep competitor research and study done and like not just produce SEO kind of content, but in, see, I understand as a very deeply technical guy and AI researcher, I'm not like just an ordinary devil who started with a very non-technical background.
I've done AI research, I've studied language and vision modality alignment, and before that I was a full stock software engineer.
So I really understand a lot of this game of agentic reasoning, agentic search.
And how do you, and how these LLMs actually authenticate you and authorize you to be able to suggest that indeed they should put you over others.
And one of the very, very key elements of this is novelty.
Like, if that, a novelty immediately leads to demand, which means like whenever people see that novel content, they immediately read it.
So LLMs, no matter how clever somebody can like do over-engineer stuff, they really cannot trick the game because these LLMs are smart.
So I'm saying the only way out of this I'm seeing is you actually have what it takes to do that thought leadership content pieces in conjunction with obviously the SEO type content.
Yeah, for sure.
The truth is like, it's not our bread and butter, you know, it's something that we can do, but it comes later in the partnership.
It comes when we get to a place where our artifacts, our understanding of your business, our knowledge base, where it's in a place.
It's like that can execute on something that's going to be novel, where we're going to drive the most value is if there is SEO opportunity against your competition, right?
And also like GEO opportunity, we can create content that is not going to be, you know, super novel and opinionated, but is the highest quality answer to those questions that your ICP has.
So I think that is, you know, just me being as honest as I can, because I'm not sure about the fit here, to tell you the truth, right?
Like, I don't want you guys to expect something that we couldn't deliver on out of the gate.
But I know that, you know, we've created like Webflow's integrations pages, we've created some of their docs, those are highly technical, these augment code examples, right?
Like, you see that there's code snippets in this content.
So we have the capability of doing...
we we have of doing...
So

Stuff that isn't just bread and butter SEO content, but there's got to be an opportunity on the SEO side first, unless this was going to be just a custom engagement from the start and the price tag, you were comfortable with going a lot higher than 15 because some of the clients we work with are paying for that level of customization from what we're doing with every new client we bring on.
And that there's a lot of demand around, frankly.
So I hope that helps.
Like, I'm not trying to be a sales guy.
Like, I'm just trying to, like, help you do your due diligence the right way.
And, you know, I'm happy to, like, answer any questions that you have.
Talk to Marcel about this use case even and get you more clarity.
But I do have to run real soon.
I'm, like, losing track of time and super late.
Yeah, what do you call it?
A list of those technical experts in your team.
you.
So that I can, like, check their accolades just to have more faith into, like, oh, yeah, they are super technical and they can produce this kind of content.
Yeah.
And Tyler, thanks for your time.
I know we are out of time.
If you can send us the information that Ankit mentioned, and also you mentioned the case study, and maybe we can do follow-up for the demo.
Yeah, for sure.
Sounds great.
Ankit, honestly, like, I would say, look at the employees at GrowthX on LinkedIn.

So I'm sorry, I got to run, but it's great meeting you, and I appreciate the time.
See ya.
Thank you.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Reconnect on a potential programmatic SEO partnership.](https://fathom.video/share/kJGFt_WEeynP2T8uCf1Jw8x45F3UUevU?tab=summary&timestamp=19.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**LandingAI's Pivot:** The company is now focused on its successful agentic document extraction API, targeting both developers and business decision-makers.](https://fathom.video/share/kJGFt_WEeynP2T8uCf1Jw8x45F3UUevU?tab=summary&timestamp=128.0)
  - [**GrowthX's Core Service:** The offering is a focused, end-to-end content engine for SEO/AEO, not a full-service agency. The initial 12-month contract is a "leap of faith" required to build a long-term organic engine.](https://fathom.video/share/kJGFt_WEeynP2T8uCf1Jw8x45F3UUevU?tab=summary&timestamp=1437.0)
  - [**Strategic Mismatch:** GrowthX's initial focus on high-quality answers to existing search demand conflicts with LandingAI's need for novel, thought-leadership content to stand out in a crowded, technical market.](https://fathom.video/share/kJGFt_WEeynP2T8uCf1Jw8x45F3UUevU?tab=summary&timestamp=1688.0)
  - [**Next Steps:** Tyler will send proof points (case studies, technical examples). A follow-up demo is planned to explore if GrowthX's capabilities can meet LandingAI's specific needs.](https://fathom.video/share/kJGFt_WEeynP2T8uCf1Jw8x45F3UUevU?tab=summary&timestamp=1987.0)

## Topics

### LandingAI's Context & Needs

  - [**Company Pivot:** Shifted from computer vision to agentic document extraction, an API product with strong market reception.](https://fathom.video/share/kJGFt_WEeynP2T8uCf1Jw8x45F3UUevU?tab=summary&timestamp=128.0)
  - [**Target Audience:**](https://fathom.video/share/kJGFt_WEeynP2T8uCf1Jw8x45F3UUevU?tab=summary&timestamp=825.0)
      - [**Developers:** Enterprise users processing high document volumes.](https://fathom.video/share/kJGFt_WEeynP2T8uCf1Jw8x45F3UUevU?tab=summary&timestamp=846.0)
      - [**Business Decision-Makers:** Seeking process optimization and insights.](https://fathom.video/share/kJGFt_WEeynP2T8uCf1Jw8x45F3UUevU?tab=summary&timestamp=846.0)
  - [**Content Goal:** Build a growth plan for the new document extraction product, aligning with a website refresh in the next two months.](https://fathom.video/share/kJGFt_WEeynP2T8uCf1Jw8x45F3UUevU?tab=summary&timestamp=171.0)

### GrowthX's Service & Model

  - [**Core Offering:** An end-to-end content engine for SEO/AEO, focused on getting clients "in the answer" of AI search.](https://fathom.video/share/kJGFt_WEeynP2T8uCf1Jw8x45F3UUevU?tab=summary&timestamp=257.0)
  - [**Service Scope:**](https://fathom.video/share/kJGFt_WEeynP2T8uCf1Jw8x45F3UUevU?tab=summary&timestamp=413.0)
      - [**Included:** Editorial & programmatic content (strategy, creation, publishing, monitoring).](https://fathom.video/share/kJGFt_WEeynP2T8uCf1Jw8x45F3UUevU?tab=summary&timestamp=290.0)
      - [**Excluded (Initially):** Link building, social media, paid media, sales lead gen.](https://fathom.video/share/kJGFt_WEeynP2T8uCf1Jw8x45F3UUevU?tab=summary&timestamp=532.0)
      - [**Add-ons:** Custom workstreams (e.g., newsletters) are possible after 3–6 months for an additional $9k–$15k/mo.](https://fathom.video/share/kJGFt_WEeynP2T8uCf1Jw8x45F3UUevU?tab=summary&timestamp=714.0)
  - [**Pricing & Structure:**](https://fathom.video/share/kJGFt_WEeynP2T8uCf1Jw8x45F3UUevU?tab=summary&timestamp=650.0)
      - [**Phase 1 (8-week Strategy Sprint):** $30k. Delivers a technical SEO audit, content roadmap, and 5–10 articles to calibrate quality.](https://fathom.video/share/kJGFt_WEeynP2T8uCf1Jw8x45F3UUevU?tab=summary&timestamp=664.0)
      - [**Phase 2 (12-month Growth Execution):** $15k/mo. Delivers 5 net new or refreshed articles per week.](https://fathom.video/share/kJGFt_WEeynP2T8uCf1Jw8x45F3UUevU?tab=summary&timestamp=664.0)
  - [**Team & Reporting:**](https://fathom.video/share/kJGFt_WEeynP2T8uCf1Jw8x45F3UUevU?tab=summary&timestamp=1159.0)
      - [**Team:** Engagement Manager (POC), Managing Editor, and Managing Directors (for strategic support).](https://fathom.video/share/kJGFt_WEeynP2T8uCf1Jw8x45F3UUevU?tab=summary&timestamp=944.0)
      - [**Reporting:** A Looker dashboard tracks sessions/conversions; Scrunch tracks AI search metrics (answers, citations, sentiment).](https://fathom.video/share/kJGFt_WEeynP2T8uCf1Jw8x45F3UUevU?tab=summary&timestamp=1159.0)

### Strategic Mismatch: Content Quality vs. Novelty

  - [**LandingAI's Concern:** The need for novel, thought-leadership content to stand out in a competitive space (vs. Google, Amazon, Microsoft).](https://fathom.video/share/kJGFt_WEeynP2T8uCf1Jw8x45F3UUevU?tab=summary&timestamp=1688.0)
  - [**GrowthX's Position:**](https://fathom.video/share/kJGFt_WEeynP2T8uCf1Jw8x45F3UUevU?tab=summary&timestamp=1844.0)
      - [Initial focus is on creating the *best answer* to existing search demand, not necessarily novel content.](https://fathom.video/share/kJGFt_WEeynP2T8uCf1Jw8x45F3UUevU?tab=summary&timestamp=1860.0)
      - [Novelty is a later-stage capability, requiring a deep knowledge base built over time.](https://fathom.video/share/kJGFt_WEeynP2T8uCf1Jw8x45F3UUevU?tab=summary&timestamp=1844.0)
      - [Technical content (e.g., code snippets) is possible but requires a clear SEO opportunity first.](https://fathom.video/share/kJGFt_WEeynP2T8uCf1Jw8x45F3UUevU?tab=summary&timestamp=1890.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Tyler:**](https://fathom.video/share/kJGFt_WEeynP2T8uCf1Jw8x45F3UUevU?tab=summary&timestamp=1987.0)
      - [Send proof points: case studies and technical content examples (e.g., Augment Code).](https://fathom.video/share/kJGFt_WEeynP2T8uCf1Jw8x45F3UUevU?tab=summary&timestamp=1594.0)
      - [Schedule a follow-up demo of GrowthX's AI workflows.](https://fathom.video/share/kJGFt_WEeynP2T8uCf1Jw8x45F3UUevU?tab=summary&timestamp=1673.0)
  - [**Ankit & Grace:**](https://fathom.video/share/kJGFt_WEeynP2T8uCf1Jw8x45F3UUevU?tab=summary&timestamp=1987.0)
      - [Review proof points and team profiles on LinkedIn to assess technical expertise.](https://fathom.video/share/kJGFt_WEeynP2T8uCf1Jw8x45F3UUevU?tab=summary&timestamp=1974.0)

action_items.description: Email Grace & Ankit: content examples, proof points, case studies
Check w/ team re: Wapi/Jordan examples; update Ankit & Grace
Schedule follow-up demo w/ Grace & Ankit re: AI workflows
